message:
Last winter, a surprise ice storm knocked out power for three days, and I watched my family eat cereal with *almond milk* heated on the camping stove because we had zero shelf-stable options. My partner kept saying, 'At least we have cereal!' Meanwhile I'm thinking about all those videos I *meant* to watch about having backup supplies.

Turns out there's something weirdly hilarious about unpreparededness—until there isn't. Now when a storm warning pops up, I actually *like* the little ritual of checking the flashlights, topping off water, and making sure the pantry has some non-perishables that don't require electricity or a camping stove.

It's not about becoming a doomsday prepper. It's just… being the person in my house who won't be standing in front of an open fridge at midnight wondering what to eat. 😄

What's your go-to comfort snack you keep on hand for power outages? (We're team peanut butter now.)
